title: AD Taylor-tower P6: exact-coefficient towers -- rational/bignum Taylor coefficients for zero-floating-error high-order derivatives
goal: Implement docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md section 9 (Phase P6). Realize the COEFF_RATIONAL coefficient-type flag reserved in esh_taylor_t.flags (PR #147): coefficients stored as tagged values (int64/bignum/rational via Eshkol's existing exact numeric tower) instead of raw doubles. (taylor f x k) and (derivative-n f x k) on polynomial/rational f with an exact x return EXACT results (n!*c_n computed exactly, arbitrary precision), following R7RS exactness contagion (any inexact input/operator -> the whole tower demotes to double, exactly like today). File boundary: runtime_taylor.c's exact-coefficient branch + the seeding/extraction/dispatch for exact mode; explicitly NOT the JET8 dispatch (P3) or multivariate/GUW layers (P4).
acceptance: derivative-n of x^5 at x0=2 (exact integer point) returns EXACT integers 32,80,160,240,240,120,0,0,0 for n=0..8, verified with `=` on exact values and `(exact? ...)` => #t, via both a plain multiply chain and (expt x 5) (exercises the exact non-negative-integer-power recurrence)
rational function 1/(1-x) at x0=1/2 (exact rational point) returns exact rational/integer derivatives matching the n!*2^(n+1) pattern, verified with exact `=` compare

bignum-scale case: x^20 at x0=3 stays exact through every order n=0..20 (n>=17 already exceeds INT64_MAX, exercising genuine arbitrary-precision bignum arithmetic, not just int64); a second x^30 @ x0=7 case forces a true (non-int64-fitting) bignum result and still matches Scheme's own bignum `expt` bit-for-bit

promotion case: a transcendental primitive (exp, or exp mixed with polynomial terms) applied to an exact tower gracefully and correctly demotes to the COEFF_F64 double tower -- numerically correct, `(inexact? ...)` => #t -- R7RS-consistent, documented
an inexact (double) seed point is unaffected: derivative-n/taylor on a double x0 still returns inexact doubles exactly as before P6 (no accidental universal-exactness regression)

ALL existing double-tower tests unchanged: taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52, taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441, taylor_tower_noheap_bench.esk no-heap invariant holds, run_ad_depth.sh gate PASS (0 regressions), AD oracle 18/18 (quick) / gate PASS, SICP smoke 88/88 -- all under both -r (JIT) and AOT
campaign_payoff: Zero floating error for arbitrary-order derivatives of polynomial/rational functions at exact points, with arbitrary-precision (bignum) magnitude -- a capability no double-based AD system (including JAX jet) offers, reusing Eshkol's existing exact numeric tower rather than adding new machinery.

delivered.representation: inc/eshkol/eshkol.h: ESH_TAYLOR_COEFF_RATIONAL=1 added to the already-reserved COEFF_MASK field. When set, esh_taylor_t's `double c[]` flexible-array storage is reinterpreted as `eshkol_tagged_value_t c[order_k+1]` (each an exact int64/bignum/rational tagged value) -- safe because the array's offset (8 bytes in, after order_k/flags) is already 8-byte aligned, matching alignof(eshkol_tagged_value_t). eshkol_taylor_alloc_exact() allocates and zero-fills this layout.
delivered.exact_arithmetic_dispatch: lib/core/runtime_taylor.c: exact_add/sub/mul/div route pure-integer add/sub/mul through eshkol_bignum_binary_tagged (arbitrary precision, int64<->bignum auto promotion/demotion) and division / any-rational-operand through eshkol_rational_binary_tagged_ptr (int64-limited exact rational substrate); a genuine (int64-overflowing) bignum meeting the rational substrate gracefully degrades that one scalar to double (documented, matches transcendental-promotion policy) rather than corrupting the pointer.
delivered.recurrences: tre_add/sub/mul/div mirror the existing tr_* kernels operation-for-operation but dispatch every multiply-accumulate through the exact substrate instead of fma(); taylor_pow_exact implements a non-negative-integer exponent via exact binary exponentiation (repeated series multiplication) instead of the general real-exponent recurrence's division, so integer powers (x^5, x^20, x^30) stay exact without ever touching the transcendental path. taylor_materialize_exact_or_demote performs the section-9 contagion check: if any resulting coefficient overflowed to double, the WHOLE tower is rebuilt as COEFF_F64 (never mixed-tagged).
delivered.dispatch_integration: eshkol_taylor_binary_tagged/eshkol_taylor_unary_tagged gate on operand_is_exact_for_taylor (same-epoch exact tower, exact scalar, or a foreign-epoch exact tower's lifted exact c[0]); add/sub/mul/div/non-negative-integer-pow stay exact, everything else (real/negative pow, or any exp/log/sin/cos/tan/sqrt/sinh/cosh/tanh -- all transcendental, all unary) falls through unchanged to the existing F64 kernel, which now correctly demotes a same-epoch EXACT tower operand via normalise_operand's exact-aware branch (previously would have raw-memcpy'd tagged_value_t bytes as doubles -- fixed as part of this phase since it is now a reachable path).
delivered.seeding_extraction: eshkol_taylor_seed_tagged inspects the point's actual exactness (int64/bignum/rational, or an outer EXACT tower's exact c[0]) and seeds a COEFF_RATIONAL tower when exact, else the unchanged COEFF_F64 seed. New eshkol_taylor_extract_tagged (arena, tower*, n, out*) computes n!*c[n] through the exact substrate and writes a genuinely exact tagged result (so `(exact? (derivative-n f x n))` is #t); eshkol_taylor_coeffs_list now emits each coefficient's own tagged value (exact or double) instead of force-casting to double. eshkol_taylor_extract (legacy raw-double) and eshkol_taylor_shift also made exact-tower-safe.
delivered.codegen_wiring: lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp: (1) popAndExtractForward's DERIV_N branch now calls eshkol_taylor_extract_tagged via a tagged out-param instead of eshkol_taylor_extract (raw double), preserving exactness end-to-end. (2) tryMonomorphizedTaylor (P2's no-heap stack-double tier) now bails to the unchanged P1 runtime path whenever the point is not PROVABLY a raw double at compile time (previously silently SIToFP'd an integer point to double, or accepted an already-boxed tagged value unconditionally) -- the monomorphized tier is stack-double-only and cannot represent exact coefficients, so exactness-bearing points must flow through the runtime tower. (3) codegenDerivativeMonolith's shared point-evaluation preamble: when adTowerMode_ != NONE (Taylor-tower mode only -- the order<=2 jet path is untouched, never sets adTowerMode_), an integer point is packed as an exact tagged int64 (packInt64) instead of being SIToFP'd to double, so it reaches eshkol_taylor_seed_tagged with its exactness intact.
delivered.tests: tests/ad/exact_taylor_test.esk (68 checks): x^5 @ x0=2 via mul-chain and expt (n=0..8, exact `=` + exact?), taylor coefficient list exactness, 1/(1-x) @ x0=1/2 (n=0..10, exact rational/integer pattern n!*2^(n+1)), x^20 @ x0=3 (n=0..20, spans into true bignum territory at n>=17), x^30 @ x0=7 (forced true bignum), exp(2) and mixed x^2+sin(x) promotion-to-double correctness, and a double-x0 regression guard (still inexact).

remainder: Rational coefficients remain int64-numerator/denominator-limited (Eshkol has no bignum-backed rational type yet); a rational op whose numerator/denominator would overflow int64 gracefully demotes to double rather than crashing or corrupting -- documented, matches the design's stated 'exact-coeff blowup' risk mitigation.

Exact pow only covers non-negative-integer, compile-time-or-runtime-CONSTANT exponents (the exponent tower must not itself depend on the differentiation variable); a same-epoch exponent tower falls back to the existing (inexact) general recurrence -- an intentionally narrow, documented scope limit not exercised by any current test.

The compile-time-K monomorphized (P2) tier never carries exact coefficients by construction (it is stack-double-only); this is an architectural choice, not a gap -- exactness always routes through the runtime/heap tower, which is the only tier that can hold heap-allocated tagged coefficients.

A dedicated `taylor-exact?` convenience predicate (mentioned in the design's API-surface sketch) was not added as a new builtin; the existing `exact?`/`inexact?` predicates already answer this directly on derivative-n/taylor results and list elements (e.g. `(exact? (car (taylor f x k)))`), so no new runtime plumbing was required.

verifications.evidence: exact_taylor_test.esk: 68/68 PASS under both -r and AOT. x^5 @ x0=2: exact integers 32,80,160,240,240,120,0,0,0 for n=0..8 via both mul-chain and expt, exact?=#t, and taylor-coeff*n! matches. 1/(1-x) @ x0=1/2: exact n!*2^(n+1) for n=0..10. x^20 @ x0=3: exact for n=0..20 (n=17..20 exceed INT64_MAX, e.g. n=17 -> 10948059036794880000, matched bit-for-bit against Scheme's own bignum expt/quotient/fact). x^30 @ x0=7: true bignum (7^30 = 22539340290692258087863249) matched exactly. exp(2) and x^2+sin(x): correctly demote to inexact double, matching libm reference within tolerance, inexact?=#t. double x0=2.0 case: still inexact as before P6 (no regression). Regressions: taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52 (-r+AOT); taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441 (-r+AOT, bit-exact mono==runtime unaffected since mono now correctly declines exact-bearing points rather than mis-converting them); taylor_tower_noheap_bench.esk no-heap invariant holds (mono arena-delta=0); run_ad_depth.sh full sweep gate=PASS (0 regressions, 12 improvements carried over from P2 baseline); run_ad_oracle.sh --quick 18/18 PASS; run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88 PASS.
